target.title: feat(slack): add opt-in admin actions (channel create, user lookup, invite, members)
target.body:
## Summary

Adds four Slack workspace admin actions behind a new opt-in gate, plus the matching MCP bridge surface so harnesses can call them once the host operator turns them on.

This is part 2 of #74757 (admin actions). It depends on #86246 (the Slack MCP bridge) for the agent-callable surface; the channel-side wiring also stands on its own (other consumers of `listSlackMessageActions` see the new actions when the gate is on).

### What lands

Channel side (`extensions/slack`):
- `actions.admin: boolean` slot on `SlackAccountSchema` / `SlackConfigSchema`, default `false`. Defining `channels.slack.actions.admin: true` per account opts in.
- `extensions/slack/src/admin-actions.ts` with four helpers (`createSlackConversation`, `lookupSlackUserByEmail`, `inviteSlackUsersToConversation`, `listSlackChannelMembers`). Each pulls the bot token first and falls back to the user token only when `userTokenReadOnly === false`.
- Dispatcher in `extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.ts` routes the new agnostic actions (`channel-create`, `user-lookup-by-email`, `addParticipant`, `member-list`) and refuses to dispatch with `Slack admin actions are disabled.` when the gate is off.
- `listSlackMessageActions` advertises them only when the gate is on, so message-tool discovery and other channel consumers stay clean by default.
- Two new entries on `CHANNEL_MESSAGE_ACTION_NAMES` (`member-list`, `user-lookup-by-email`); the other two reuse existing semantic names (`channel-create`, `addParticipant`).

Bridge side (`src/agents/tools/slack-tool.ts`):
- `SLACK_TOOL_ACTIONS` gains `createConversation`, `lookupUserByEmail`, `inviteUsers`, and `listMembers`.
- Schema picks up `name`, `isPrivate`, `email`, `userIds`, and `cursor`.
- `buildSlackActionRequest` maps each one to its agnostic action name, with required-parameter validation (`name`, `email`, `channelId`+`userIds`, `channelId`).
- Tool description states the admin opt-in and continues to keep `apps.manifest.create` and other workspace-level operations explicitly out of scope.

Docs:
- `skills/slack/SKILL.md` documents the admin actions with example calls and the `channels.slack.actions.admin: true` opt-in note. The skill stays gated by `requires.config: ["channels.slack"]` so it is only loaded when Slack is configured.
- `CHANGELOG.md` Unreleased entry.

### Why this shape

- **Default off**: bot accounts in the wild don’t usually carry `channels:manage`, `groups:write`, `users:read.email`, etc. Auto-advertising these would either fail at runtime or silently expand authority on hosts where the bot does have them. The opt-in is per account, so a multi-workspace setup can turn it on only where the scopes are granted.
- **No new SecretRef**: `userToken` is already a `SecretInputSchema` field, and `userTokenReadOnly` is already the existing opt-in for letting the bot use user-context writes. Reusing them avoids carving out a parallel admin-token surface and lets the same `channels.slack.actions.admin` gate work whether the bot or the user token is doing the work.
- **One agent surface**: instead of N MCP tools, the existing `slack` bridge gains four extra `action` enum values, keeping the prompt footprint flat and the discovery story identical to the rest of the Slack actions.

### Tests

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/tools/slack-tool.test.ts \
  src/mcp/openclaw-tools-serve.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/message-tools.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/channel.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/channel-actions-setup-status.contract.test.ts \
```

target.title: fix(gateway): dedupe probe warnings by gateway identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw gateway probe` warned about multiple reachable gateways when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes reached the same gateway through different transports.
- Solution: classify reachable probe warnings by gateway self identity plus logical gateway port instead of raw reachable target count.
- What changed: gateway self presence now parses legacy `Gateway: host (ip)` text, and status warnings suppress duplicate same-identity transports while preserving distinct/ambiguous warnings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): unknown identity probes still warn conservatively, and same-host probes on different gateway ports still warn.

## Motivation

- Avoid misleading remote-gateway diagnostics for a valid setup where both SSH tunnel and direct LAN URL point to one gateway.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69135
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `gateway probe` no longer emits `multiple_gateways` for two reachable transports that identify the same gateway and same logical gateway port.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout using a Linux network namespace as the remote host, with a temporary sshd, real `/usr/bin/ssh` local port forward, direct remote WebSocket URL, and paired `operator.read` device auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts --reporter=dot`; then `node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway probe --ssh root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port> --ssh-identity <temp-key> --timeout 20000 --json` against a namespace-hosted gateway with `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`.
- Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 2 files and 10 tests. The live CLI proof returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `warnings: []`, `primaryTargetId: "sshTunnel"`, and two reachable targets: `sshTunnel` at `ws://127.0.0.1:<gateway-port>` and `configRemote` at `ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, both reporting the same gateway self identity and `operator.read` scope.
- Observed result after fix: same gateway identity plus same gateway port produces no `multiple_gateways` warning; distinct or ambiguous reachable probes still produce the warning.
- What was not tested: an external physical remote host; the live proof used an isolated Linux network namespace to model the remote host while still exercising `/usr/bin/ssh` and the real CLI probe path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildGatewayStatusWarnings` treated `reachable.length > 1` as multiple gateways without comparing the gateway self identity returned by each probe.
- Missing detection / guardrail: output tests did not cover multiple reachable probe targets that represent the same gateway through different transports.
- Contributing context (if known): SSH tunnel probing prepends a local tunnel URL while keeping the configured remote URL, so two reachable targets can be one logical gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts`, `src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: same-identity reachable transports suppress `multiple_gateways`; distinct, unknown, or different-port gateways still warn.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in warning construction after probe results are already collected.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw gateway probe` no longer shows the multiple-gateways warning when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes identify the same gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [reachable count > 1] -> multiple_gateways

After:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [same self identity + gateway port] -> no warning
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway status/probe output
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.mode=remote`, `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, `gateway.remote.sshTarget=root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port>`, paired device auth with `operator.read`; remote gateway bound on LAN in a Linux netns.

### Steps

1. Run focused unit tests for SSH tunnel/configured remote warning behavior.
2. Start a temporary SSH daemon and OpenClaw gateway inside an isolated Linux network namespace.
3. Configure the probe home with both a direct remote WebSocket URL and an SSH target to that namespace.
4. Run `openclaw gateway probe --ssh ... --json` and verify both transports reach the same gateway identity with no `multiple_gateways` warning.

### Expected

- Same identity and same logical port: no `multiple_gateways`.
- Distinct or ambiguous gateway identities: keep `multiple_gateways`.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: same identity suppression; same-host different-port warning; distinct identity warning; unknown identity warning; legacy self-presence parsing; live CLI probe with real SSH tunnel plus configured direct remote URL to the same namespace-hosted gateway.
- Edge cases checked: SSH tunnel identity uses remote gateway port instead of local tunnel port; direct remote and tunnel targets both returned the same `self` host/IP/version/platform; `warnings` was empty.
- What you did **not** verify: an external physical remote host; the namespace proof covers the same network shape in-process on WSL.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: suppressing the warning too broadly could hide a real multi-gateway setup.
  - Mitigation: the warning remains for unknown identities, distinct host/IP identities, and same-host targets on different gateway ports.

target.title: fix(config): skip shell env fallback on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84795 by making the optional login-shell env fallback a no-op on native Windows before it can resolve or execute `/bin/sh`.

The existing POSIX behavior is unchanged. `getShellPathFromLoginShell` already skipped `win32`; this applies the same platform boundary to the direct `loadShellEnvFallback` path used during config loading.

## Verification

2026-05-24 native Windows proof from this branch, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`:

- Native Windows Node: `v22.22.3`
- Built a local smoke bundle from this branch with native Windows esbuild:
  `node_modules\@esbuild\win32-x64\esbuild.exe src\entry.ts --bundle --platform=node --format=esm --packages=external --outfile=.\oc85739-entry.mjs`
- Ran the affected startup/status path with shell-env fallback enabled:
  `$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'; $env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'; node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status`
  - exit code: `0`
  - stderr: empty
  - stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT`: `False`
  - stdout includes `OpenClaw status` and `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`
- Direct fallback proof from the same native Windows checkout also returned:
  `{ "platform": "win32", "result": { "ok": true, "applied": [] }, "warnings": [] }`

2026-05-23 update after rebasing onto `origin/main` (`acf265d4d5`), head `8ef82383f5`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/shell-env.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (1 file, 21 tests passed)
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`

Earlier verification before the rebase:

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 21 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: With `OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1` or config-driven shell env import enabled on native Windows, OpenClaw should not try to execute `/bin/sh` and should not emit `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed: spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`.

Native Windows environment tested: Windows 10.0.26200 x64, Node `22.22.3`, checkout at `C:\src\openclaw-85739-win` on branch `fix-84795-win32-shell-env-fallback`, head `fde7096e11e8b21dc474d1f9ceac3ad330432e33`.

Exact affected command run after this patch:

```powershell
$env:OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV='1'
$env:OPENCLAW_HOME='C:\tmp\oc85739-home'
node .\oc85739-entry.mjs status
```

Evidence after fix: the command exited `0`, stderr was empty, stdout started with `OpenClaw status`, and a captured stdout scan for `/bin/sh|ENOENT` returned `False`. The status output reported `OS windows 10.0.26200 (x64) · node 22.22.3`, proving the smoke ran on native Windows rather than WSL.

Observed result after fix: native Windows status starts normally, the shell-env fallback does not attempt `/bin/sh`, and no ENOENT fallback warning is emitted. Direct module proof of `loadShellEnvFallback` on the same machine also returned `{ ok: true, applied: [] }` with no warnings.

What was not tested: I did not run the globally installed `openclaw` package. The smoke used a local bundle from this PR checkout because this Windows machine intermittently denies pnpm/esbuild postinstall child-process shims, while the native Windows entry bundle and Node runtime execute correctly.

target.title: perf(models-config): content-hash auth-profiles + models.json drift detection
target.body:
## Summary

Splits the cache-fingerprint half of #72869 into a standalone PR.

This PR replaces mtime-based cache key inputs with content-based hashes for the `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` implicit-provider-discovery cache, plus a second-factor models.json content hash that catches external edits / partial corruption / sibling-process writes between cache hits. Includes the security hardening findings raised by Aisle on the original combined PR.

## Why content hashes

The previous mtime-based key invalidated on every OAuth token refresh because `auth-profiles.json` gets rewritten with new access/refresh timestamps even when the set of available providers does not change. Same for `models.json` mtime: the file is the OUTPUT of this function, so each call observed its own write and invalidated the next call.

Now:

- **auth-profiles.json**: SHA-256 over a stable serialization that strips volatile OAuth session fields (`access`, `refresh`, `expires*`, `issuedAt`, `refreshed/lastChecked/lastRefresh/lastValidatedAt`). Token rotation no longer invalidates; structural changes (added/removed profiles, rotated static `type:token` credentials) still do.
- **models.json**: NOT included in the input fingerprint (would cause self-invalidation). Instead its content hash is captured at write time and stored alongside the readyCache entry. Every cache check recomputes the file hash and compares; any external edit invalidates and forces re-plan.

## Security hardening (Aisle review on PR #72869)

| Severity | Finding | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| 🟠 High #1 | CWE-59 symlink-following chmod | `ensureModelsFileMode` now lstats first; refuses to chmod symlinks or non-regular files |
| 🟡 Med #3 | CWE-1321 prototype pollution | `Object.create(null)` for stripped result + explicit `__proto__`/`prototype`/`constructor` filter |
| 🟡 Med #4 | DoS via unbounded fingerprinting | `MAX_AUTH_PROFILES_BYTES = 8 MiB` (raw-hash above cap), `MAX_AUTH_PROFILES_DEPTH = 64` with depth-marker |
| 🟡 Med #5 | CWE-312 secrets in cache | `buildModelsJsonFingerprint` returns SHA-256 hex of canonical payload instead of raw stable-stringified |

## Token-rotation correctness

`token` is intentionally NOT in `AUTH_PROFILE_VOLATILE_FIELDS` even though OAuth-style `token` fields rotate. Profiles with `type: "token"` use the literal `token` key as a long-lived static credential, and stripping it would mask real auth-state changes when a user rotates that credential. Documented inline.

## Tests

Two tests in `models-config.fingerprint-cache.test.ts`:
- `does not invalidate the cache when OAuth session fields rotate` (rotates access/refresh/expires; cache stays valid)
- `DOES invalidate the cache when a static type:token credential rotates` (rotates the literal `token` field; cache invalidates)

Existing fingerprint-cache test suite still passes.

## Compatibility

`MODELS_JSON_STATE.readyCache` value shape extended with `modelsJsonHash`: `{ fingerprint, modelsJsonHash, result }`. All three plan return paths (skip/noop/write) capture the post-write hash. The `refreshedFingerprint` re-key path forwards `modelsJsonHash` through unchanged.

## Related

- Splits out of #72869 (cache-fingerprint half)
- The targetProvider short-circuit half is in #73261

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Models-config cache fingerprint fail-closed behavior for unhashable/oversize `models.json` and `auth-profiles.json` from PR #73260.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw topic branch `perf/models-config-cache-fingerprint` at `05fda3d839`, isolated temporary agent directory, production `ensureOpenClawModelsJson` invoked through a `tsx` runtime driver with no Vitest mocks.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the proof driver after the patch to warm cache on a small auth profile, grow `auth-profiles.json` past the 8 MiB cap, repeat calls with byte-identical and swapped oversize contents, restore a small file, then exercise oversize and symlinked `models.json`.
- **Evidence after fix**: Full copied runtime trace is in the PR comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73260#issuecomment-4384857383

  Excerpt of copied live output:
  ```text
  ensureOpenClawModelsJson warm cache hit: ~1 ms
  auth-profiles.json > 8 MiB: re-plan ~150-200 ms on every call
  oversize byte-identical repeat: re-plan, cache size unchanged
  oversize same-byte-length swapped content: re-plan, cache size unchanged
  restored small auth-profiles.json: cache hit restored (~1 ms)
  oversize models.json: full plan + rewrite
  symlinked models.json: full plan + rewrite
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: Uncacheable models content never matches cached state, oversize auth profiles bypass the ready cache instead of collapsing to a stale fingerprint, and restoring cacheable content re-enables fast hits.
- **What was not tested**: Nothing else for this PR's changed behavior beyond the isolated local runtime proof and supplemental automated validation.

## Current-head token-profile expiry proof (2026-05-12)

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Token-profile expiry invalidation for PR #73260. A static `type: "token"` profile changing from valid to expired must change the models-config ready-cache fingerprint; OAuth session-field rotation, including `expires`, must remain volatile and keep the same cache key.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw topic branch `perf/models-config-cache-fingerprint` at `1d1e11d64d`, isolated temporary agent directories under `/tmp`, production `ensureOpenClawModelsJson`, production `MODELS_JSON_STATE.readyCache`, real on-disk `auth-profiles.json` and `models.json`; no Vitest and no mocks of the models-config cache seam.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-73260-token-profile-expiry.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```text
  Proof root: /tmp/openclaw-73260-token-proof-AsCf46
  [1/3] oauth-session-fields-are-volatile
      cache key stayed stable: dd54f163d6f5ace2
  [2/3] token-expiry-invalidates-cache
      valid-token key:   576ba05d6b45bd0f
      expired-token key: 623b9d4bb64832e5
  [3/3] expired-token-cache-stabilizes
      cache keys after repeat expired call: 2

  All runtime assertions passed.
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: OAuth access/refresh/expiry rotation kept one stable cache key, while static token expiry valid-to-expired produced a distinct cache key and then reused that expired-state key on a repeat call.
- **What was not tested**: Live provider network discovery. The proof intentionally scopes provider discovery to an empty provider-id list so it can exercise the production cache/fingerprint/runtime file behavior without external network calls.

target.title: perf(plugins): thread prepared manifestPlugins through runtime model-id normalize chain
target.body:
## Summary

Extends the prepared-manifest-metadata threading from #82814 into the runtime plugin-normalize branch so callers that already carry prepared `manifestPlugins` skip the residual `installs.json` slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. Closes the gap that #82814's measurements left visible (Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` was at 28 metadata scans per cold+warm sample post-#82814).

Builds on the architecture shakkernerd endorsed when closing #78589: carrying prepared facts through the hot consumers rather than adding another lazy slot-refill path. Does not touch `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` itself.

## Why

`#82814` (`fix: reuse plugin manifest metadata safely`, merged 2026-05-17) threaded prepared `manifestPlugins` through the static model-id normalize path (`normalizeStaticProviderModelId` in `src/agents/model-ref-shared.ts`) so consumers stop consulting the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` single-slot handoff in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. That eliminated most of the hot-path `installs.json` disk walks: Kova metadata scans dropped 394 → 28 per cold+warm sample (~93% reduction).

The remaining 28 scans come from the runtime plugin-normalize branch. `src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts` already receives `options.manifestPlugins` from its caller and threads it into the static branch (line 51-54); the runtime branch immediately below (line 58-66) silently drops it, and the chain falls through to the slot-or-load disk walk:

\`\`\`
src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59
  → normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime(...)         (no plugins param)
    → normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin(params)     (no plugins param)
      → normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params) (params.plugins undefined)
        → loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies   (slot-or-load disk walk)
\`\`\`

`normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest` already honors `params.plugins` to short-circuit:

\`\`\`ts
function loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params) {
  if (params.plugins) {
    return collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins);  // prepared path
  }
  const { snapshot, cacheable } = resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies(params);  // slot-or-load
  // ...
}
\`\`\`

The intermediate functions just never accepted a `plugins` field. This PR adds it as an additive optional param and forwards `options?.manifestPlugins` from the existing call site that already has it in scope.

## How

Three surgical edits:

1. **`src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts`** — add optional `plugins?: readonly Pick<PluginManifestRecord, \"modelIdNormalization\">[]` to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithRuntime`'s params. The body just forwards `params` unchanged to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`, so no behavioral change beyond the type signature.

2. **`src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`** — add the same optional `plugins?` field to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithPlugin`'s params. The existing `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest(params)` call already forwards everything, so `plugins` flows straight through to where it's honored.

3. **`src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts:59`** — at the runtime call site, thread `options?.manifestPlugins` into the runtime call. Spread-conditional so the call shape is unchanged when `manifestPlugins` is absent.

Net diff: +6 LOC across three source files (plus tests and changelog).

## Relationship to the previously closed PR #78589

`#78589` proposed a different fix at a different layer: refilling the `current-plugin-metadata-snapshot` slot after a cold load inside `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`. shakkernerd closed it 2026-05-17 as superseded by #82814 with the framing: *current `main` now avoids the release-blocking path by carrying prepared manifest/plugin metadata through the hot model/runtime/session consumers rather than relying on another lazy republish point* — and invited follow-up: *If there is still a distinct empty-slot case after current main, please reopen with current-main repro steps, the exact SHA, and trace/timing evidence for the remaining path.*

This PR is that follow-up, scoped to the residual gap with a fix that **extends the consumer-threading pattern shakkernerd endorsed** rather than reintroducing the slot-refill approach. The original `archive/perf-manifest-model-id-lazy-publish` git tag in `zeroaltitude/openclaw` preserves the prior branch for reference.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: Callers that pass prepared `manifestPlugins` to `normalizeModelRef` (via `ModelManifestNormalizationContext`) had that metadata silently dropped when the runtime plugin-normalize branch was taken, causing the chain to fall through to the slot-or-load disk walk in `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` even though prepared metadata was available in the caller's scope.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw worktree against `origin/main` at `03125c8e13` (synced from `upstream/main`), branch `perf/manifest-model-id-thread-runtime-plugins`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/agents/model-selection-manifest-workspace.test.ts src/agents/model-selection.test.ts src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/plugins/manifest-model-id-normalization.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/provider-model-normalization.runtime.ts src/agents/model-selection-normalize.ts src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: tsgo:core exit 0; tsgo:core:test exit 0; vitest 9 files / 349 tests passed; oxlint exit 0.
- **Observed result after fix**: With prepared `manifestPlugins`, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: [...] }` and the production chain hands them through to `normalizeProviderModelIdWithManifest`, where the existing `params.plugins` short-circuit in `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` takes the prepared path and skips `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies` entirely. Without prepared plugins, the runtime call shape is byte-identical to current `main` (regression test pins this).
- **What was not tested**: I have not run the full Kova `agent-cold-warm-message` repro on a deployed-style mock provider environment to measure the 28 → ~3 scan reduction in production scale. The mechanism is identical to #82814's: when `params.plugins` is present, `loadManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies` returns immediately from `collectManifestModelIdNormalizationPolicies(params.plugins)` without invoking `resolveMetadataSnapshotForPolicies`, so the savings scale 1:1 with how often the runtime plugin-normalize branch is hit on cold-slot turns. Maintainers with the Kova harness can re-run that scenario; happy to add that measurement if you can point me at the canonical fixture.

## Tests

Added in `src/agents/model-selection.plugin-runtime.test.ts`:

- `forwards manifestPlugins to the runtime normalization call so it can skip the slot-or-load disk walk` — asserts that when `normalizeModelRef(..., { manifestPlugins: prepared })` is called, the runtime mock receives `{ plugins: prepared, ... }`.
- `omits plugins from the runtime call when no manifestPlugins are prepared (preserves current behavior)` — asserts the call shape is unchanged for the no-prepared-metadata path.

All existing test cases in the affected files continue to pass.

## Verification

- `tsgo:core`: clean
- `tsgo:core:test`: clean
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs <5 affected files>`: 9 files / 349 tests passed
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs <4 affected source files>`: 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check`: clean
- Branched from `origin/main` (= `upstream/main` at `03125c8e13`); no rebase needed at submission.

target.title: fix(outbound): thread sessionKey into message_sending + align session.key with agent runtime + document the contract
target.body:
## What

Three layers of one bug:

1. **Plumbing fix.** `applyMessageSendingHook` in `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` constructed the `PluginHookMessageContext` with only `{ channelId, accountId, conversationId }` and dropped the `sessionKey` that `deliverOutboundPayloads` already had in scope as `sessionKeyForInternalHooks` (resolved at deliver.ts:952 from `mirror.sessionKey ?? session.key`). Plugins observing `message_sending` saw `ctx.sessionKey === undefined` for every reply.

2. **Semantic fix.** `dispatch-from-config.ts:488` was filling `session.key` with `ctx.SessionKey` unconditionally. For non-native chat that's correct (matches what the agent runner uses); for native-command-redirect (`CommandTargetSessionKey` set), the agent runs against the redirect target, so `session.key` must follow it too. Otherwise `agent_end` and `message_sending` see different sessionKeys for the same turn. Now mirrors `get-reply.ts:198`'s `agentSessionKey = targetSessionKey || ctx.SessionKey`.

3. **Contract documentation.** Tightened JSDoc on `OutboundSessionContext.key`/`policyKey` and `PluginHookMessageContext.sessionKey`/`runId` so the agent_end ↔ message_sending correlation invariant is documented, and added explicit comments in `deliver.ts` distinguishing the diagnostics fallback (`?? policyKey` allowed) from the internal-hook fallback (`?? policyKey` deliberately disallowed).

## Concrete regression observed

A non-bundled plugin (`openclaw-provenance`) correlates a per-turn signal across two hooks:

- `agent_end` writes to a per-session map keyed by `ctx.sessionKey`.
- `message_sending` looks up the same key and appends a developer-mode taint footer to outbound content.

Pre-fix, message_sending's ctx.sessionKey was `undefined` (issue 1) so the lookup always missed; post-plumbing-fix but pre-semantic-fix, the lookup would also miss for any native-command-redirect flow (issue 2). Both regressions are addressed; downstream plugins now have a stable invariant (issue 3).

## Tests

`pnpm test src/infra/outbound/deliver.test.ts` → 56/56 (53 prior + 3 new). New tests cover:
- sessionKey threaded into ctx when session is provided
- sessionKey absent when session is absent
- session.key (canonical) wins over session.policyKey in the hook ctx

`tsgo --noEmit` typecheck clean.

## Risk

Low. The plumbing fix is additive (`sessionKey?: string` field already declared in the hook context type). The semantic fix only changes behavior for the native-command-redirect path, which was already broken (no plugin could correlate across hooks there). JSDoc/comment updates are documentation-only. Five files modified at narrow points.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Canonical outbound `sessionKey` is threaded into `message_sending` / `message_sent` hook context, including native redirect session-key selection from PR #73706.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw topic branch `fix/message-sending-session-key` at `862bb66603`, real `deliverOutboundPayloads`, real `getGlobalHookRunner`, real registered hooks, real channel plugin path via `scripts/proof-73706-message-sending-session-key.ts`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran `pnpm tsx scripts/proof-73706-message-sending-session-key.ts` after the patch and captured the runtime hook context values received by the registered hooks.
- **Evidence after fix**: Full copied runtime output is in the proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/73706#issuecomment-4384869283 and saved locally at `~/reports/proof-73706/run-output.txt`.

  Excerpt of copied live output:
  ```text
  pnpm tsx scripts/proof-73706-message-sending-session-key.ts
  scenario: direct outbound delivery
  message_sending ctx.sessionKey = agent:proof:direct
  message_sent ctx.sessionKey = agent:proof:direct
  scenario: native redirect target
  message_sending ctx.sessionKey = agent:proof:redirect-target
  message_sent ctx.sessionKey = agent:proof:redirect-target
  proof: PASS
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: The actual runtime hook context contains the canonical delivery session key for outbound hooks; native redirect delivery uses the redirect target key instead of the inbound session key.
- **What was not tested**: `runId` outbound hook correlation is documented as not yet plumbed and was not claimed/tested by this PR.

target.title: Guard OpenAI chat payload turns
target.body:
Fixes #86145

## Summary
- Refuse OpenAI-compatible chat streams when compaction/transport transforms leave a system/tool-only payload with no user or assistant turns.
- Classify blank prompt submissions with system/tool-only replay as empty history instead of replay-backed blank user prompts.
- Add regressions for the stream-time guard and compaction branch recovery of user/assistant turns when preparation only contains tool output.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Qwen/OpenAI-compatible chat requests no longer reach the provider with a `messages[]` payload that has no user or assistant turn after compaction and transport transforms.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw runtime on this PR branch, using `createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn` against a real local HTTP/SSE endpoint that behaves like an OpenAI-compatible `/v1/chat/completions` server.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with a script that first sends a positive-control user turn to the loopback OpenAI-compatible endpoint, then sends a system-only runtime prompt through the same stream function.

Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the runtime proof run:

```json
{
  "proof": "PR #86177 OpenAI-compatible runtime guard",
  "runtime": "real local OpenAI-compatible HTTP/SSE endpoint via createOpenAICompletionsTransportStreamFn",
  "model": "mlx/mlx-community/Qwen3-30B-A3B-6bit",
  "endpoint": "http://127.0.0.1:[REDACTED_PORT]/v1",
  "positive_control": {
    "requests_received": 1,
    "captured_roles": ["system", "user"],
    "done_reason": "stop"
  },
  "system_only_guard": {
    "requests_before": 1,
    "requests_after": 1,
    "network_requests_delta": 0,
    "error_event_seen": true,
    "stop_reason": "error",
    "error_message": "OpenAI-compatible chat payload for mlx/mlx-community/Qwen3-30B-A3B-6bit contains no user or assistant messages after compaction and transport transforms; refusing to send a system/tool-only request. Start a new user turn or repair the compacted session history."
  },
  "result": "PASS: system-only OpenAI-compatible payload was caught locally before HTTP send"
}
```

Observed result after fix: The positive-control request reached the loopback endpoint and captured `messages` roles `["system", "user"]`, proving the runtime endpoint was live and normal OpenAI-compatible sends still reach the network. The system-only run emitted the new local error, and the loopback endpoint request count stayed unchanged (`network_requests_delta: 0`), showing the malformed payload was blocked locally before `chat.completions.create` sent an HTTP request to the endpoint.

What was not tested: No external paid or remote Qwen provider call was made. This proof uses a real local OpenAI-compatible HTTP/SSE endpoint to show the transport guard fires before network send; maintainers should still decide whether the user/assistant-free payload invariant should remain global or be provider-compat scoped.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.prompt-helpers.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.prompt-helpers.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.prompt-helpers.test.ts src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

If this PR is squashed or reworked, please preserve Andy Ye as the author or include:
Co-authored-by: Andy Ye <35905412+TurboTheTurtle@users.noreply.github.com>

target.title: fix(discord): default bare numeric IDs to channel kind in target resolver
target.body:
## Summary

When the message-tool sends to a bare numeric Discord channel ID (e.g. `273512430271856640`) without a `preferredKind` hint, the `resolveTarget` callback in `channel.ts` passes an empty options object `{}`. `parseDiscordTarget` then throws "Ambiguous Discord recipient" because no `defaultKind` is set.

Every other bare-numeric-ID call site in the Discord extension already defaults to `channel`:
- `normalizeDiscordMessagingTarget` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `resolveDiscordChannelId` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `resolveDiscordTargetChannelId` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `inferTargetChatType` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `normalizeDiscordOutboundTarget` -> adds `channel:` prefix

This aligns the `resolveTarget` callback to do the same.

Closes #86001

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/discord/src/channel.ts` | When `preferredKind` is undefined, pass `{ defaultKind: "channel" }` instead of `{}` |
| `extensions/discord/src/targets.test.ts` | Add test for bare numeric ID resolution with `defaultKind: "channel"` |

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: bare numeric Discord channel IDs no longer throw "Ambiguous Discord recipient" when sent via message-tool

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24, pnpm test full Discord extension suite

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm test extensions/discord
```

Evidence after fix:
```
$ pnpm test extensions/discord

 ✓ |extension-discord| extensions/discord/src/targets.test.ts (19 tests) 37ms
   ... (including new "resolves bare numeric ids as channels" test)

 Test Files  148 passed (148)
 Tests  1509 passed (1509)
```
The `resolveTarget` callback now passes `{ defaultKind: "channel" }` for all cases where `preferredKind` is not `"user"`, matching the pattern used by all 5 other bare-numeric-ID handlers in the extension.

Observed result after fix: `parseDiscordTarget("273512430271856640", { defaultKind: "channel" })` returns `{ kind: "channel", id: "273512430271856640", normalized: "channel:273512430271856640" }` instead of throwing. User-prefixed IDs (`user:123`, `<@123>`) continue to resolve as users.

What was not tested: live Discord API call (no bot token in test environment)

target.title: Repair anchorless iMessage watch payload routing
target.body:
## Summary
- recover malformed anchorless iMessage watch payloads by GUID before debounce/routing
- drop unrecoverable anchorless payloads fail-closed instead of routing them to the sender DM
- add unit coverage for the repair/drop helper plus a monitor-level route assertion that the final dispatch target is the recovered group

## Why
A group link-preview watch payload can arrive with `chat_id=0`, empty conversation identifiers, and `is_group=false`. Without a pre-routing repair/drop guard, OpenClaw can treat the payload as a direct message and send the reply to `imessage:<sender>` instead of the originating group.

Closes #84470.
Refs #84503.

Credit: this follows the bug report and mitigation direction from @zqchris and the contributor work in #84503 by @zhangguiping-xydt, but reapplies the fix on current `main` with broader anchorless detection and route-level assertions.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.last-route.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.watch-subscribe-retry.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.gating.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm lint:extensions:bundled`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check CHANGELOG.md extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/conversation-repair.test.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor/monitor-provider.ts extensions/imessage/src/monitor.last-route.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Live iMessage proof
Ran on the Lobster Mac against the live `imsg rpc` bridge from this PR branch (`c89bbe2862`). The probe used `chats.list` and `messages.history`, built a synthetic anchorless payload from a real group-history message, and called the same repair helper added by this PR.

Repair proof, redacted:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "sourceWasAnchorless": true,
  "repairedWasAnchorless": false,
  "groupCount": 3,
  "selectedChatId": 18,
  "repairedChatId": 18,
  "sourceGuid": "A3294...CCF2E5",
  "repairedChatGuid": "any;+...b7e6aa",
  "repairedIsGroup": true,
  "participantsRecovered": 8
}
```

Fail-closed proof with a non-existent anchorless GUID, redacted:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "sourceWasAnchorless": true,
  "result": "dropped",
  "diagnosticCount": 1
}
```

## Re-verified locally with a fast-patched gateway (Lobster Mac)

Re-ran end-to-end against a different real group (3 participants) and exercised the wired-in code path on a live gateway.

Harness against the live `imsg rpc` bridge (3 cases):

| Case | Input | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Positive | Real recent group message, anchor fields stripped, real `guid` preserved | Recovered `chat_id`, `chat_guid`, `is_group=true`, 3 participants in **2 RPC calls** (`chats.list` + 1 `messages.history`) |
| Negative | Anchorless payload with a fabricated bogus GUID | Dropped (`null`) after scanning 10 chats; no fallback DM to sender |
| Pass-through | Payload with valid anchor | Returned identical; **0 RPC calls** — no overhead on the happy path |

Live gateway regression — fast-patched this branch's dist into the running gateway, then sent a real iPhone message into a 3-person test group:

```
imessage inbound: chatId=13 from=imessage:group:13 len=114 preview="…"
message received: channel=imessage chatId=chat_id:13 messageId=299
  sessionKey=agent:lobster:imessage:group:13 source=dispatchInboundMessage
message queued: queueDepth=1
session state: prev=idle new=processing
session turn created: runId=… channel=imessage
agent harness selected
```

Anchored payload took the repair wrapper's pass-through path — no `recovered anchorless` / `dropping anchorless` log entries — and end-to-end routing was unchanged.

target.title: [Fix] #86151 [Bug]: openclaw still appending "-preview" to "gemini-3.1-flash-lite" in v2026.5
target.body:
Fixes #86151

### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

target.title: [Fix] #86199 TUI Esc abort can leave stale optimistic busy state
target.body:
Fixes #86199

## Summary

The TUI can remain stuck in the busy-submit path after pressing Esc to abort a pending run. The first Esc successfully aborts the backend run, but the local optimistic submit flag remains set, so the next normal prompt is blocked with:

```text
agent is busy — press Esc to abort before sending a new message
```

Pressing Esc again then reports:

```text
no active run
```

because both tracked run ids are already clear while `pendingOptimisticUserMessage` is still true.

target.title: fix(docker): set node ownership on /home/node/.config parent directory
target.body:
## Summary

`install -d` creates missing parent directories implicitly, but applies `-o`/`-g`/`-m` flags only to the leaf directories listed on the command line. When `/home/node/.config/openclaw` was created, the intermediate parent `/home/node/.config` was auto-created as `root:root` with default umask (`0755`). This prevents the `node` user from creating sibling directories under `.config` at runtime.

Closes #85968

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `Dockerfile` | Add `/home/node/.config` to the explicit `install -d` directory list and its `stat` verification |
| `scripts/docker/setup.sh` | Add explicit `chown node:node /home/node/.config` for existing containers |
| `src/dockerfile.test.ts` | Assert `/home/node/.config` appears in both the `install -d` command and the stat check |

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `/home/node/.config` directory in the slim Docker image is now owned by `node:node` with mode `0700` instead of `root:root` with mode `0755`

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24, Dockerfile build-time stat guard + unit tests

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm test src/dockerfile.test.ts
pnpm test src/docker-setup.e2e.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
$ pnpm test src/dockerfile.test.ts

 ✓ |unit-fast| src/dockerfile.test.ts (19 tests) 13ms
   ✓ pre-creates named-volume mount points before switching to the node user

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  19 passed (19)

$ pnpm test src/docker-setup.e2e.test.ts

 ✓ src/docker-setup.e2e.test.ts (34 tests) 2642ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  34 passed (34)
```
The Dockerfile build-time `stat -c '%U:%G %a' /home/node/.config | grep -qx 'node:node 700'` guard makes the fix self-verifying: if the parent directory has wrong permissions, the Docker build itself will fail. The existing `stat` checks for `.openclaw`, `.openclaw/workspace`, and `.config/openclaw` continue to pass.

Observed result after fix: the `install -d` command now explicitly lists `/home/node/.config` alongside the three existing directories, and the `stat` guard verifies `node:node 700` for all four directories. The `setup.sh` chown step now also covers the parent directory for existing containers.

What was not tested: Docker image build on Linux CI (verified via build-time stat guard pattern and Dockerfile test assertions; the stat guard will fail the build if permissions are wrong)

target.title: feat(mcp): bridge slack channel actions through openclaw-tools MCP server
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a thin MCP bridge so Claude Code (and any other harness restricted to `mcp__openclaw__*` tools) can call the Slack actions that `skills/slack/SKILL.md` already documents.

Today Slack is registered through `defineBundledChannelEntry`, which means it never enters the bundle/MCP tool catalog (`src/plugins/bundle-config-shared.ts` only keeps `record.format === "bundle"`), and Claude Code is launched with `--allowedTools "mcp__openclaw__*"` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-backend.ts`). The skill lists actions like `react`/`sendMessage`/`pinMessage`, but the agent has no callable tool — a footgun the maintainer review explicitly called out in #74757.

This PR re-exposes the existing Slack channel-plugin actions through the OpenClaw-tools MCP server (the same path used by `cron`):

- `react`, `reactions`
- `sendMessage`, `editMessage`, `deleteMessage`, `readMessages`
- `pinMessage`, `unpinMessage`, `listPins`
- `memberInfo`, `emojiList`
- `uploadFile`, `downloadFile`

The tool is a parameter-mapper only: it forwards to the gateway `message.action` method with `channel: "slack"`, so account scoping, credentials, and dispatch logic stay in the channel plugin. The agent surface stays a single tool (`slack`) with a typed `action` enum — same shape Claude Code already expects from `mcp__openclaw__*` entries.

Admin operations like `conversations.create`, `users.lookupByEmail`, and `apps.manifest.create` are intentionally **out of scope** of this PR. Those need new tokens (`userToken`, `appConfigToken`) and an explicit opt-in story; they will land in follow-up PRs (issue #74757 part 2).

This corresponds to direction **(a)** from the issue update (MCP bridge over the existing channel plugin), which the issue thread settled on as the smallest reviewable shape. The complementary skill-loading guard (direction **(b)**) is a separate small PR.

## Changes

- `src/agents/tools/slack-tool.ts`: new agent tool that maps the action enum + parameters to the existing Slack channel plugin contract and dispatches via `callGatewayTool("message.action", …)`. Each action enforces its required parameters; default 60s timeout; idempotency key per call.
- `src/mcp/openclaw-tools-serve.ts`: register `createSlackTool()` next to `createCronTool()` in `resolveOpenClawToolsForMcp()`.
- `src/agents/tools/slack-tool.test.ts`: 19 unit tests covering action-to-channel-action mapping, account scoping, send-target precedence (`to` over `channelId`), validation errors, and that the tool is not `ownerOnly`.
- `src/mcp/openclaw-tools-serve.test.ts`: assert the MCP catalog lists `slack` with the full action enum.
- `skills/slack/SKILL.md`: short note that harnesses on `mcp__openclaw__*` reach the same surface as `mcp__openclaw__slack` through this bridge, and that admin operations stay out of scope.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: Unreleased entry under Changes.

## Tests

```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/tools/slack-tool.test.ts \
  src/mcp/openclaw-tools-serve.test.ts
# 40 passed, 3 files

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/slack/src/message-tools.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/channel.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/message-action-dispatch.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/action-runtime.test.ts \
  extensions/slack/src/channel-actions-setup-status.contract.test.ts \
  src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts
# 155 passed, 6 files (existing Slack channel suite still green)
```

oxlint and oxfmt clean on the touched files.

## Notes for maintainers (@steipete)

Posting this as the answer to the direction question on #74757 — Q1 = (a) MCP bridge, Q2 = admin ops stay default-off and land in a separate PR series, Q3 = starting now rather than waiting; happy to iterate on this and the follow-up PRs.

Refs #74757

target.title: fix(launcher): recognize Bun module-not-found errors without ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND code
target.body:
## Summary

Bun's `ResolveMessage` errors carry `"Cannot find module"` in the message but do not set the Node-specific `code: "ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND"`. The launcher's `isModuleNotFoundError` predicate gated entirely on that code, so under Bun the `catch` blocks in `installProcessWarningFilter` and `tryImport` re-threw instead of continuing, causing a crash loop when `dist/warning-filter.js` was missing.

This widens the predicate to also match `"Cannot find module"` and `"Cannot find package"` in the error message, matching the pattern already used by the browser extension (`extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-ai-module.ts:10-23`). The inner `isDirectModuleNotFoundError` caller already narrows to exact-specifier matching, so transitive dependency errors are still surfaced correctly.

Closes #86198

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `openclaw.mjs:250-260` | Widen `isModuleNotFoundError` to accept Bun-style errors via message matching |

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Bun launcher crash loop when `dist/warning-filter.js` is missing and Bun throws without `ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND` code

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24, full launcher e2e suite

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm test test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
$ pnpm test test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts

 ✓ test/openclaw-launcher.e2e.test.ts (22 tests) 3438ms
   ✓ surfaces transitive entry import failures instead of masking them as missing dist
   ✓ keeps the friendly launcher error for a truly missing entry build output

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
 Tests  22 passed (22)
```
The transitive-error-surfacing test (line 174) confirms that widening the outer gate does not mask transitive failures. The missing-entry test (line 193) confirms direct module misses still produce the friendly error. Both critical safety tests pass unchanged.

The predicate change is also validated by code inspection: `isDirectModuleNotFoundError` (lines 253-269) already does exact-specifier matching via URL comparison and path-in-message checks, so the outer gate only determines "is this a module resolution error at all?" while the inner function determines "is it for THIS specific specifier?"

Observed result after fix: `isModuleNotFoundError({ message: "Cannot find module './dist/warning-filter.js'" })` returns `true` even without `code: "ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND"`, allowing the launcher to fall through gracefully under Bun.

What was not tested: live Bun runtime (Bun is not installed in the test environment; the error format difference was verified by reading Bun source and issue reports)

target.title: fix(discord): default bare numeric IDs to channel kind in target resolver
target.body:
## Summary

When the message-tool sends to a bare numeric Discord channel ID (e.g. `273512430271856640`) without a `preferredKind` hint, the `resolveTarget` callback in `channel.ts` passes an empty options object `{}`. `parseDiscordTarget` then throws "Ambiguous Discord recipient" because no `defaultKind` is set.

Every other bare-numeric-ID call site in the Discord extension already defaults to `channel`:
- `normalizeDiscordMessagingTarget` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `resolveDiscordChannelId` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `resolveDiscordTargetChannelId` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `inferTargetChatType` -> `{ defaultKind: "channel" }`
- `normalizeDiscordOutboundTarget` -> adds `channel:` prefix

This aligns the `resolveTarget` callback to do the same.

Closes #86001

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/discord/src/channel.ts` | When `preferredKind` is undefined, pass `{ defaultKind: "channel" }` instead of `{}` |
| `extensions/discord/src/targets.test.ts` | Add test for bare numeric ID resolution with `defaultKind: "channel"` |

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: bare numeric Discord channel IDs no longer throw "Ambiguous Discord recipient" when sent via message-tool

Real environment tested: macOS 15.5, Node 24, pnpm test full Discord extension suite

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
pnpm test extensions/discord
```

Evidence after fix:
```
$ pnpm test extensions/discord

 ✓ |extension-discord| extensions/discord/src/targets.test.ts (19 tests) 37ms
   ... (including new "resolves bare numeric ids as channels" test)

 Test Files  148 passed (148)
 Tests  1509 passed (1509)
```
The `resolveTarget` callback now passes `{ defaultKind: "channel" }` for all cases where `preferredKind` is not `"user"`, matching the pattern used by all 5 other bare-numeric-ID handlers in the extension.

Observed result after fix: `parseDiscordTarget("273512430271856640", { defaultKind: "channel" })` returns `{ kind: "channel", id: "273512430271856640", normalized: "channel:273512430271856640" }` instead of throwing. User-prefixed IDs (`user:123`, `<@123>`) continue to resolve as users.

What was not tested: live Discord API call (no bot token in test environment)

target.title: Prevent plain text tool call leaks
target.body:
## Summary
- Promote standalone provider text like `[tool:read] { ... }` into structured tool calls before the xAI tool stream wrapper runs.
- Share that plain-text tool-call promotion wrapper with LM Studio so xAI and LM Studio cannot drift on parser limits, event shape, or sanitizer behavior.
- Teach the shared tool payload parser and user-facing/outbound sanitizers about `[tool:name]` pseudo-tool syntax, then strip/reject leaked tool-call-only sends before Discord can echo raw internal tool syntax back into chat.

## Testing
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/lmstudio/src/stream.test.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: xAI/Grok plain-text tool-call fallbacks such as `[tool:message] { ... }` are promoted into structured tool calls or stripped from channel-visible output before Discord delivery; LM Studio now uses the same shared promotion wrapper instead of a duplicate provider-local implementation.
- Real environment tested: OrbStack Docker gateway for the live @Spartacus Discord bot in the LibraVDB server, using `xai/grok-4.3` on the `openclaw:spartacus-latest-main-hotfix` image with host ports `18889/18890`; current branch commit `293f9f9f08` was also tested locally through the xAI, LM Studio, Plugin SDK parser/export, outbound sanitizer, and send-validation code paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18889/healthz`
  - `docker logs --tail 80 openclaw-orbstack-bot-openclaw-gateway-1`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/lmstudio/src/stream.test.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18889/healthz
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}

$ docker logs --tail 80 openclaw-orbstack-bot-openclaw-gateway-1
[gateway] agent model: xai/grok-4.3 (thinking=low, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (3 plugins: discord, duckduckgo, libravdb-memory; 2.9s)
[discord] channels resolved: 1488730235544273049 (guild:LibraVDB; aliases:guild:1488730235544273049)
[discord] [default] Discord bot probe resolved @Spartacus

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/lmstudio/src/stream.test.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  47 passed (47)
```

- Observed result after fix: The live gateway stayed healthy, connected to Discord, and used the expected xAI/Grok runtime; the focused stream tests proved both xAI and LM Studio convert matching fallback text into `toolcall_start`, `toolcall_delta`, and `done(reason="toolUse")` events instead of forwarding raw `[tool:...]` text, while the outbound sanitizer/send-validation coverage rejects tool-call-only message bodies.
- What was not tested: I did not post a new public Discord message from this follow-up commit because the code change only migrates LM Studio onto the shared helper; the xAI/Discord runtime was verified through live health/log output plus the focused xAI stream and outbound delivery tests.

target.title: fix(cli): preserve Discord voice outbound helper
target.body:
Summary
- Keep CLI lazy outbound dependency routing from treating Discord voice helper keys as normal channel adapters.
- Preserve explicitly supplied `discordVoice` / `sendDiscordVoice` helpers while leaving missing helpers unresolved so the Discord runtime can decide the voice path.
- Add focused regression coverage for the CLI deps proxy and outbound send mapping, plus a changelog fix entry for #84952.

Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/cli/deps.ts src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --parallel-tests "node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts"`
- `git diff --check`

Behavior addressed: Discord cron announces with `messages.tts.auto="always"` could fail before the Discord voice-message helper ran because the CLI outbound deps proxy manufactured a fake channel adapter for the `discordVoice` helper lookup.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu source checkout on Node 22 with focused CLI, Discord outbound adapter, and cron double-announce regression tests. Crabbox/live Discord delivery was not run for this source-level fix.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/deps.test.ts src/cli/outbound-send-mapping.test.ts extensions/discord/src/outbound-adapter.test.ts src/cron/isolated-agent/delivery-dispatch.double-announce.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The new CLI deps regression asserts `createDefaultDeps().discordVoice` and `createDefaultDeps().sendDiscordVoice` remain `undefined` instead of becoming unavailable channel adapters, and the outbound mapping regression asserts lazy channel factories are not called for those helper keys while explicit helpers are still preserved.

Observed result after fix: Focused regression suite passed: 4 files, 106 tests passed. Formatting, lint, diff check, and autoreview also passed.

What was not tested: A live Discord cron announce with generated TTS audio was not run before opening this PR; the proof is focused source-level regression coverage for the broken dependency lookup path.

Fixes #84952

## Summary
- prevent cron tool auto-injection of caller session key when a cron job explicitly targets `sessionTarget: "isolated"`
- make `cron.remove` return an invalid-request error when the target job id is not found, instead of `ok: true, removed: false`

Fixes #86202

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/cron.validation.test.ts`
- autoreview clean after rebase on `origin/main`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: isolated cron jobs should not inherit the caller/dashboard session key, and removing a missing cron id should be surfaced as an invalid request instead of a successful no-op.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on Node 24-compatible tooling, loading the real cron tool and gateway cron handler modules through the repo test harness.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/tools/cron-tool.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/cron.validation.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files 4 passed (4)` and `Tests 204 passed (204)`. The new cron-tool assertion observed an isolated add payload with `sessionTarget: "isolated"` and no `sessionKey`; the gateway validation assertion observed `INVALID_REQUEST` for a missing `cron.remove` id.

Observed result after fix: isolated cron add requests remain isolated, and missing cron removals now return a clear invalid-request error.

What was not tested: a full long-running gateway cron scheduler process with wall-clock job execution; the changed request construction and gateway handler branches were tested directly.

## Summary

- route `openclaw node status` recovery hints for missing-unit and stopped-runtime states through stdout
- keep the primary status/error summary on stderr so scripts can still detect the failure state
- add focused coverage for both affected node status branches

Fixes #83925.

## Verification

2026-05-24 update after rebasing onto current `main`, head `2fd3cd10ca`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/node-cli/daemon.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 1 file, 2 tests passed

target.title: Prevent plain text tool call leaks
target.body:
## Summary
- Promote standalone provider text like `[tool:read] { ... }` into structured tool calls before the xAI tool stream wrapper runs.
- Share that plain-text tool-call promotion wrapper with LM Studio so xAI and LM Studio cannot drift on parser limits, event shape, or sanitizer behavior.
- Teach the shared tool payload parser and user-facing/outbound sanitizers about `[tool:name]` pseudo-tool syntax, then strip/reject leaked tool-call-only sends before Discord can echo raw internal tool syntax back into chat.

## Testing
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/lmstudio/src/stream.test.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/sync-plugin-sdk-exports.mjs --check`
- Node 24.14.0: `node scripts/check-plugin-sdk-subpath-exports.mjs`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: xAI/Grok plain-text tool-call fallbacks such as `[tool:message] { ... }` are promoted into structured tool calls or stripped from channel-visible output before Discord delivery; LM Studio now uses the same shared promotion wrapper instead of a duplicate provider-local implementation.
- Real environment tested: OrbStack Docker gateway for the live @Spartacus Discord bot in the LibraVDB server, using `xai/grok-4.3` on the `openclaw:spartacus-latest-main-hotfix` image with host ports `18889/18890`; current branch commit `293f9f9f08` was also tested locally through the xAI, LM Studio, Plugin SDK parser/export, outbound sanitizer, and send-validation code paths.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18889/healthz`
  - `docker logs --tail 80 openclaw-orbstack-bot-openclaw-gateway-1`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/lmstudio/src/stream.test.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ curl -fsS http://127.0.0.1:18889/healthz
{"ok":true,"status":"live"}

$ docker logs --tail 80 openclaw-orbstack-bot-openclaw-gateway-1
[gateway] agent model: xai/grok-4.3 (thinking=low, fast=off)
[gateway] http server listening (3 plugins: discord, duckduckgo, libravdb-memory; 2.9s)
[discord] channels resolved: 1488730235544273049 (guild:LibraVDB; aliases:guild:1488730235544273049)
[discord] [default] Discord bot probe resolved @Spartacus

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/lmstudio/src/stream.test.ts extensions/xai/stream.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/tool-payload.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-stream-shared.test.ts
Test Files  4 passed (4)
Tests  47 passed (47)
```

- Observed result after fix: The live gateway stayed healthy, connected to Discord, and used the expected xAI/Grok runtime; the focused stream tests proved both xAI and LM Studio convert matching fallback text into `toolcall_start`, `toolcall_delta`, and `done(reason="toolUse")` events instead of forwarding raw `[tool:...]` text, while the outbound sanitizer/send-validation coverage rejects tool-call-only message bodies.
- What was not tested: I did not post a new public Discord message from this follow-up commit because the code change only migrates LM Studio onto the shared helper; the xAI/Discord runtime was verified through live health/log output plus the focused xAI stream and outbound delivery tests.

target.title: fix(docker): create /home/node/.config with node ownership
target.body:
## Summary

- Fix `/home/node/.config` ownership in the Docker slim image to `node:node` instead of `root:root`.
- The `install -d` command creates intermediate directories with root ownership by default, since `-o`/`-g` flags only apply to the final target directory.
- Added stat assertions for `/home/node/.config` and the Dockerfile guard test to prevent regression.

Fixes #85968

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/dockerfile.test.ts`
- `docker run --rm node:24-bookworm-slim sh -ceu 'install -d -m 0755 -o node -g node /home/node/.config && install -d -m 0700 -o node -g node /home/node/.openclaw /home/node/.openclaw/workspace /home/node/.config/openclaw && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.openclaw | grep -qx "node:node 700" && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.openclaw/workspace | grep -qx "node:node 700" && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.config/openclaw | grep -qx "node:node 700" && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.config | grep -qx "node:node 755"'`
- autoreview clean after rebase on `origin/main`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Docker runtime images should let the non-root `node` user write standard config under `/home/node/.config`, while keeping OpenClaw's private state directories at mode 0700.

Real environment tested: real `node:24-bookworm-slim` container, matching the OpenClaw runtime base image family.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `docker run --rm node:24-bookworm-slim sh -ceu 'install -d -m 0755 -o node -g node /home/node/.config && install -d -m 0700 -o node -g node /home/node/.openclaw /home/node/.openclaw/workspace /home/node/.config/openclaw && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.openclaw | grep -qx "node:node 700" && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.openclaw/workspace | grep -qx "node:node 700" && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.config/openclaw | grep -qx "node:node 700" && stat -c "%U:%G %a" /home/node/.config | grep -qx "node:node 755"'`

Evidence after fix: the command exits 0 and `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/dockerfile.test.ts` reports `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 19 passed (19)`.

Observed result after fix: `/home/node/.config` is `node:node 755`; `/home/node/.openclaw`, `/home/node/.openclaw/workspace`, and `/home/node/.config/openclaw` remain `node:node 700`.

What was not tested: a full multi-stage OpenClaw image build; the exact directory-creation command was tested in the matching slim base image.
